archetype_reasoning: Directionality of 1.0 and open positions dominated by near-certainty entries (0.97-0.999 for obvious outcomes, 0.01-0.02 for near-impossible outcomes) are classic yield-harvesting signatures. The open book shows systematic collection of small premiums on highly probable outcomes: SNP winning Scottish Parliament seats at 0.997, Zelenskyy-Putin no-meeting at 0.989, Heather Humphreys not winning Irish presidency at 0.975. The recent catastrophic loss on Mamdani YES at 0.87-0.938 (bought a near-certainty that failed) confirms the archetype: occasional large losses when the harvested tail event materialises.
domain_blindspots: Iran_ME: 1W/3L (25% accuracy) — consistently wrong on Middle East outcomes
Europe: 11W/11L (50% accuracy) — despite heavy activity in European elections, no demonstrable edge
Trump_US: 58% accuracy is marginal and likely inflated by near-certainty positions
domain_summary: Apparent strength in the catch-all 'Other' domain (76% accuracy) likely reflects yield-harvesting on obvious outcomes rather than genuine forecasting edge. Russia_UKR accuracy (67%) is a thin sample of 9 trades and should not be over-weighted.
signal_weight_reasoning: Near-perfect directionality (1.0) and systematic near-certainty entries strip out genuine forecasting signal. When this trader does enter at contested prices (Mamdani YES at 0.87+), the result is catastrophic loss suggesting no real edge at those levels. The portfolio is structured to generate yield from obvious outcomes, not to forecast uncertain events — making it unreliable for STR-003 signal generation.
behavioural_flags: Extreme concentration: 13 separate trades on a single Mamdani market at similar near-peak prices (0.866-0.938), suggesting averaging-up or repeated position-building
Near-certainty portfolio construction: majority of open positions entered at >0.87 or <0.05 price levels
Sunk-cost doubling: multiple identical Mamdani entries at 0.938 suggesting repeated reinvestment despite no price improvement
Asymmetric position structure: small long-shot longs (Glucksmann 2.3%, Whitmer 2.1%) alongside heavy near-certainty shorts — optionality hunting on tails
Possible stale positions: Sinn Féin 2024 Irish election at 0.001 (already resolved market) still listed as open
risk_patterns: Mamdani blowup: concentrated 13+ trade loss at high prices illustrates catastrophic tail risk from yield-harvesting strategy
Romanian presidential election at 0.989 entry — lost — another near-certainty failure showing systematic underestimation of resolution uncertainty
Concentration risk: multiple open positions in Irish presidential election across different candidates without clear hedging logic
Overlapping exposure: YES on Catherine Connolly winning AND NO on Connolly winning by less than 15% creates structural confusion about actual thesis
geo_elo_active (1904) significantly decayed from base geo_elo (2033), suggesting recent performance deterioration

open_positions_summary: Portfolio is heavily oriented toward near-certainty yield collection across European politics and geopolitical low-probability events: SNP dominance in Scotland, no China-Taiwan invasion, Zelenskyy-Putin no-meeting, Trump not acquiring Greenland. A cluster of Irish presidential election positions across multiple candidates (Connolly, Humphreys) creates confused exposure. Small speculative long-shot longs on Glucksmann (2.3%), Whitmer (2.1%), and Lammy (1%) suggest optionality hunting. Overall thesis: earn steady small returns on obvious outcomes while holding tiny positions on high-upside long shots.
watch_items: Monitor for further Mamdani-style concentration events: does this trader repeat the pattern of doubling into a losing near-certainty position?
Track Irish presidential election resolution — multiple overlapping positions could generate both wins and losses simultaneously
Observe whether geo_elo_active continues to decay, indicating ongoing performance deterioration post-Mamdani blowup
Watch if the Zelenskyy-Putin no-meeting position (0.989) resolves correctly — a Putin-Zelenskyy meeting would be another severe tail hit
Monitor total PnL trajectory: $5,115 realized gains could evaporate quickly if another concentrated near-certainty fails
summary: This trader is a textbook yield-harvester operating across geopolitics and European elections, extracting small premiums from near-certain outcomes rather than making genuine probabilistic forecasts. The directionality score of 1.0 and the concentration of open positions at price extremes (>0.87 or <0.05) confirm this is a premium-collection strategy, not a forecasting one. The Mamdani debacle — 13+ trades at 0.87-0.938 on a position that resolved adversely — illustrates the catastrophic tail risk embedded in this approach and explains the significant decay in geo_elo_active (2033 base vs. 1904 active). Domain accuracy figures, especially the 76% 'Other' category, are likely artifacts of near-certainty positioning and should not be interpreted as genuine forecasting skill.
